ucs.title: [LLM] Patchwork (Dropping Elephant) Equation Editor (CVE-2017-11882) RTF weaponization chain
ucs.description: Hunts for EQNEDT32.EXE spawning script interpreters or LOLBins, the signature initial-access → execution chain Patchwork uses in spearphishes to South Asian (esp. Pakistani / Chinese) government targets. Patchwork has been one of the most persistent abusers of CVE-2017-11882 / CVE-2018-0802 RTF lures dropping BADNEWS, QuasarRAT, and BackConfig.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="EQNEDT32.EXE" AND Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","hh.exe","msiexec.exe") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process Processes.parent_process_id Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename host as dest | append [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.process_name="EQNEDT32.EXE" AND (Filesystem.file_name="*.exe" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.scr" OR Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*","*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*","*\\ProgramData\\*")) by host Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | rename host as dest] | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let suspChildren = dynamic(["powershell.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","hh.exe","msiexec.exe"]);
let eqnExec = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "EQNEDT32.EXE"
    | where FileName in~ (suspChildren)
          or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("http://","https://","-EncodedCommand","FromBase64String","DownloadString","DownloadFile","Invoke-Expression","IEX")
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessParentFileName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, SHA256;
let eqnDrop = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "EQNEDT32.EXE"
    | where FolderPath has_any (@"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Public\")
    | where FileName endswith ".exe" or FileName endswith ".dll" or FileName endswith ".scr" or FileName endswith ".lnk"
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, FolderPath, FileName, SHA256, InitiatingProcessFileName;
eqnExec | union eqnDrop | sort by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: EQNEDT32.EXE has no legitimate reason to spawn shells, script hosts, or LOLBins, nor to drop PE/script files into user-writable paths. Patchwork's playbook for nearly a decade has revolved around weaponized RTFs exploiting Equation Editor as the initial-access primitive — Cymmetria's 'Unveiling Patchwork' report, Volexity's BackConfig analysis, and Talos BADNEWS reporting all describe this exact c

ucs.title: [LLM] Patchwork BADNEWS dead-drop resolver beacon from Office-spawned interpreter
ucs.description: Hunts the BADNEWS / BackConfig C2 pattern where a script host or PowerShell descended from an Office process resolves C2 from public dead-drop platforms (GitHub, Pastebin, Blogspot, WordPress, forums) — a Patchwork-defining T1102.001 behavior used to evade static C2 indicators.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.app IN ("powershell.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe") AND All_Traffic.dest_port IN (80,443,8080) by host All_Traffic.app All_Traffic.dest All_Traffic.dest_port | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` | rename host as dest_host | join type=inner dest_host [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","eqnedt32.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe") by host Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename host as dest_host] | join type=inner dest_host [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Web where Web.url IN ("*github.com*","*githubusercontent.com*","*pastebin.com*","*blogspot.com*","*wordpress.com*","*tumblr.com*","*forum*","*feed*","*rss*") AND Web.user_agent!="Mozilla*" by host Web.url Web.user_agent Web.app | `drop_dm_object_name(Web)` | rename host as dest_host] | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let officeParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","eqnedt32.exe"]);
let interpreters = dynamic(["powershell.exe","powershell_ise.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe"]);
let deadDrops = dynamic(["github.com","raw.githubusercontent.com","gist.githubusercontent.com","pastebin.com","paste.ee","blogspot.com","wordpress.com","tumblr.com","feedburner.com","feedproxy.google.com"]);
let officeSpawn = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (officeParents)
    | where FileName in~ (interpreters)
    | project SpawnTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, ParentProc=InitiatingProcessFileName, ChildProc=FileName, ChildPid=ProcessId, ChildCmd=ProcessCommandLine;
let ddrConns = DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (interpreters)
    | where RemoteUrl has_any (deadDrops) or tostring(parse_url(RemoteUrl).Host) has_any (deadDrops)
    | project ConnTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessId;
officeSpawn
| join kind=inner (ddrConns) on DeviceId
| where ConnTime between (SpawnTime .. (SpawnTime + 1h))
| where InitiatingProcessId == ChildPid
| project SpawnTime, ConnTime, DeviceName, ParentProc, ChildProc, ChildCmd, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP
| sort by SpawnTime desc
ucs.rationale: BADNEWS — Patchwork's signature backdoor since 2015 (Forcepoint MONSOON, Talos, Volexity reporting) — uses dead-drop resolvers on GitHub, Pastebin, Blogspot, WordPress and feed services to retrieve real C2 addresses, a behavior that distinguishes Patchwork from peers like SideWinder or Donot. Joining the Office→interpreter spawn lineage with the same PID's outbound request to a dead-drop platform
